results.title: Data Science & AI Intern Job Details | Seagate Technology
highlights:
Data Science & AI Intern Job Details | Seagate Technology
[...]
# Data Science & AI Intern
[...]
Posting Date: Apr 7, 2026
[...]
Job ID: 13669
[...]
The Global Operational Intelligence Group transforms data into actionable insights to support factory operations in delivering industry-leading hard disk drive solutions. We aim to drive Seagate factory’s digitalization and intelligence by leveraging advanced analytics, machine learning (ML), and artificial intelligence (AI) to optimize operations and unlock new business value.
[...]
## About the role - you will:
[...]
- Work closely with data scientist mentors to formulate business and engineering challenges as well‑defined AI problems, developing anomaly detection (AD) and root cause analysis (RCA) solutions for complex manufacturing processes and capacity planning controls
[...]
- Apply advanced data science techniques—including time series foundation models for machine sensor AD problems, causal AI for RCA problems, and AI-driven optimization and reasoning of actionable parameters—to accurately model manufacturing behavior and enable data driven, resilient operational decision making
[...]
- Design and implement scalable data and AI systems for large‑scale manufacturing datasets, leveraging Python, advanced AI/ML modeling, and enterprise analytics platforms to deliver reliable, production‑ready insights in real operating environments
[...]
## About you:
[...]
- Passionate about AI/ML technology with a focus on delivering practical business value
- Collaborative, adaptable, and effective in team environments
- Proven ability to drive initiatives and influence key outcomes
- Strong problem-solving mindset, with organizational and time management skills
[...]
## Your experience includes:
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree or higher in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Engineering, or a related field
- Substantial experience in AI/ML technologies applied to industrial use cases, including projects involving tabular data, time series data, and computer vision, in order of importance
[...]
- Proficient in Python for model development with some experience in libraries (Polar, Scikit-learn), deep learning frameworks (PyTorch), hyperparameter tuning (Optuna), causal AI (DoWhy, PyMC), optimization and simulation (SciPy, Google OR-Tools, Evolutionary Algorithms, Mesa), GenAI-based complex reasoning (OctoTools) and evolutionary coding agents (OpenEvolve)

results.title: Seagate Recruitment 2026: Intern - Machine Learning (Gen AI) | Pune | OffCampusJobs4u.com
highlights:
# Seagate Recruitment 2026: Intern – Machine Learning (Gen AI) | Pune
[...]
## Seagate Recruitment 2026 | Intern – Machine Learning (Gen AI) | Pune
[...]
Are you a Masters student looking to revolutionize business operations using the latest in Generative AI? Seagate is officially hiring for the Intern – Machine Learning (Gen AI) position at its Pune innovation center. This Seagate Recruitment 2026 drive is a high-visibility opportunity to join a global Data Analytics group. You will work on the cutting edge of Large Language Models (LLMs) and MLOps, blending fast-changing AI technology with high-impact manufacturing analytics.
[...]
| Category | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Intern – Machine Learning (Gen AI) |
| Location | Pune, India |
| Qualification | Pursuing Masters in CS / ML / Statistics |
| Core Tech Stack | Python, SQL, PyTorch/TensorFlow, LLMs (Llama/Azure OpenAI) |
| Job ID | 13143 |
[...]
As a Machine Learning Intern at Seagate, you will be part of a core product team of 12-15 engineers and data scientists. Your primary focus will be developing and optimizing Large Language Model (LLM) based solutions. You will leverage both commercial models, like Azure OpenAI, and open-source models, like Llama, to accelerate daily workflows for end-users. This role requires a seamless integration of AI models into existing Big Data platforms and supporting infrastructure.
[...]
In addition to model development, you will design and implement tools for the entire MLOps lifecycle—including deployment, serving, monitoring, and experimentation. You will collaborate with Business SMEs and Data Engineers to ensure that deep learning solutions are scalable and efficient. Success in this role requires a detail-oriented professional who can question complex problems and propose innovative, out-of-the-box AI solutions for the HiTech and manufacturing space.
[...]
- Develop and optimize analytics solutions based on Large Language Models (LLMs).
- Integrate commercial and open-source LLMs (Azure OpenAI, Llama) into business workflows.
- Design and manage MLOps processes: development, serving, and monitoring.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to build end-to-end analytics platforms.
[...]
- Implement deep learning solutions using TensorFlow or PyTorch frameworks.
- Write efficient Python code and manage data using SQL queries.
[...]
- Participate in technical experimentation and model performance tuning.
- Maintain clear documentation for AI workflows and supporting infrastructure.
[...]
- Education: Currently pursuing a Masters in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, or related fields.
- NLP Experience: Proven hands-on or research experience in Natural Language Processing (NLP).
- AI Knowledge: Strong understanding of Generative AI tools, techniques, and ML algorithms.
- Programming: Strong background in Python and familiarity with SQL.
- Deep Learning: Experience in training and deploying models in PyTorch or TensorFlow.
- Organization: Uncompromisingly detail-oriented with solid time management skills.
- Communication: Excellent verbal and written abilities to instill commitment within a team.
[...]
Interested candidates can apply through the official Seagate Careers portal by searching for Job ID 13143. The selection process typically includes a review of your research/project portfolio in NLP, followed by technical assessments in Python and deep learning frameworks. Shortlisted students will be interviewed by the Global Data Analytics team. Ensure your resume highlights your experience with LLMs and MLOps. Click the button below to start your application.
[...]
2. What models will I work with during the internship? You will gain exposure to both commercial LLMs (like Azure OpenAI) and open-source frameworks (like Meta’s Llama).
[...]
3. Is the work focused only on model development? No, the role also emphasizes MLOps, including model serving, monitoring, and infrastructure integration.

results.title: Seagate ML Internship 2026 Pune – Apply Now
highlights:
# Seagate Internship 2026: ML Gen AI Role for Master’s Students in Pune
[...]
Seagate Technology, a global leader in data storage solutions with over 29,000 employees, is offering a high-visibility Machine Learning internship focused on Generative AI at its Pune campus. This is not a typical intern role—you will join a product team of 12-15 data scientists and ML engineers to build and optimize Large Language Model (LLM) based analytics solutions that directly impact business operations. With the rapid adoption of Gen AI across industries, this internship provides a rare opportunity to gain hands-on MLOps experience and work with cutting-edge models like Azure OpenAI and Llama in a production environment. If you are a Master’s student from the 2025 or 2026 batch with strong Python and NLP skills, this could be your gateway into the AI industry.
[...]
| Company | Seagate Technology |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Intern – Machine Learning (Gen AI) |
| Location | Pune, Maharashtra |
| Experience | Freshers / Internship |
| Education | Pursuing Master's in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics or related field |
| Salary | Not Disclosed (Competitive stipend) |
[...]
This role is open to students currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning Engineering, Statistics, or a closely related field. Both 2025 and 2026 batch students can apply. While a consistent academic record is preferred, Seagate places greater emphasis on strong fundamentals in machine learning and proven hands-on experience. Candidates should have robust Python programming skills, familiarity with SQL, and practical experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch. Crucially, you must have prior experience developing Natural Language Processing (NLP) models—this is not an entry-level requirement; recruiters will look for project work or research involving text data, transformers, or language models. Strong communication and time management skills round out the profile.
[...]
- Develop and optimize Large Language Model based analytics solutions for business operations.
[...]
- Integrate LLM models with existing analytics platforms seamlessly.
- Leverage commercial (Azure OpenAI) and open-source (Llama) models to accelerate user workflows.
[...]
- Design and implement tools for the full MLOps cycle: model development, deployment, serving, monitoring, and experimentation.
[...]
- Collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, and business subject matter experts to deliver end-to-end analytics platforms.
[...]
- Contribute to maintaining supporting infrastructure for advanced analytics and big data solutions.
[...]
- Stay current with Generative AI tools and techniques and apply them to real-world problems.
[...]
- Python
- SQL
- TensorFlow
- PyTorch
- Natural Language Processing
- Generative AI
- Large Language Models
- MLOps
- Azure OpenAI
- Llama
[...]
The selection process is structured and rigorous. Round 1 is an online application and screening—your resume and academic profile are reviewed. Shortlisted candidates proceed to Round 2, which consists of technical interviews covering machine learning principles, Python coding, NLP concepts, Generative AI, and deep learning frameworks. Expect questions on transformer architectures, fine-tuning LLMs, and model evaluation. The final Round 3 is an HR discussion focused on your interests, learning goals, and cultural fit within the Global Data Analytics team. The entire process typically takes 2-4 weeks, and given the niche skill set, candidates with solid project portfolios often advance faster.
[...]
In 202
[...]
, and Amazon are actively
[...]
Click the ‘Apply Now’ button in the job post, which redirects to the official Seagate careers page. Create an account or log in, fill in your academic details, upload your updated resume in PDF format, and submit. It is recommended to apply as soon as possible since positions fill quickly.

manufacturing/test environments.

• Work closely with relevant departments to secure customer data and financial statements • Perform credit analysis for new and existing customers and assess the credit risk associated with the proposed or assigned credit line • Perform in-depth financial analysis and provide recommendations to support management decision-making • Create ad-hoc analyses, executive summaries, and recurring reporting to support management decision-making • Monitor and analyze key economic indicators across AMER, EMEA, and APAC • Identify potential risks and opportunities within the Global customer portfolio • Perform cash flow analysis and create customer forecasts • Conduct research and conceptualize new techniques, ideas, and solutions for a variety of credit challenges • Provide relevant departments with critical updates on the financial condition of their customers • Partner with Sales and Sales Operations to support order management decisions (e.g., holds/releases) by monitoring credit exposure, past-due trends, and dispute risk • Work as one global team: coordinate and facilitate credit and collections efforts with close communication and collaboration across regions • Enforce credit policies and procedures through, but not limited to, the following: Be familiar with the policies and procedures; Maintain and update desktop procedures on a regular basis; Communicate and enforce policies when necessary; Assist with SOX controls and compliance procedures; Perform ad-hoc control procedures that may be assigned

### 🎯 Requirements

• Eager to learn and curious about what is going on in the World • Strong attention to detail and ability to notice discrepancies in data • Interested in new techniques using AI and Machine Learning to automate and improve credit analysis • Able to interpret and synthesize financial data, news, and relevant market intelligence (including third-party sources) as inputs to Seagate’s in-house credit assessment • Experience in developing mathematical and statistical models is a plus • Being proactive on taking actions on anticipated environmental changes, legislation, market movements, annual performance results of our customers, and compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements. • Basic concept of credit risk mitigation strategies, practices, and systems • Exceptional analytical skills • Knowledge in analysis of financial statements, cash flow analysis, industry/market credit research, and ever-changing country risks is a plus • Outstanding communication and presentation skills with the ability to develop customer relationships, negotiate, and effectively relay information and resolve disputes • Possess a process improvement mindset with a desire to consistently look for ways to improve efficiency and service while maintaining a “big picture” perspective • Proven initiative to support current and future growth. • Working in a credit analysis function or quantitative experience • Bachelor’s and/or equivalent experience • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, Power Point) • Excellent English verbal and written communication skills (Understanding and reading Chinese fluently is a plus) • Ability to effectively manage competing deadlines for projects, with varying degrees of supervision. • Familiarity with SAP system is a plus • Worked in larger scale corporations that have presence worldwide

results.title: Sr. Staff Program Manager - Digital Campaigns (Fremont, CA, US) | Hirequorum
highlights:
Sr. Staff Program Manager - Digital Campaigns (Fremont, CA, US) | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# Sr. Staff Program Manager - Digital Campaigns (Fremont, CA, US)

Posted Jun 1, 2026

### About our group:



Seagate Corporate Marketing drives Seagate’s position in the market. We show how Seagate’s innovations, employees, customers, and partners bring the value of data to life through owned, earned, and paid activations.

### About the role - you will:



Seagate is looking for an experienced digital campaign manager that brings content to life across the customer journey. The Sr. Staff Program Manager of Digital Campaigns will Design, execute, and optimize cross-channel digital demand and brand campaigns (search, paid social, display, programmatic, email, and media partnerships) that drive qualified pipeline for B2B and conversion/retention for B2C.

Key Responsibilities:

- Develop data-backed digital campaign programming based on Seagate and industry-level benchmarks.

- Create quarterly and annual end-to-end media plans including search, paid social, display, programmatic, email, and media partnerships.

- Work with analytics team to refine audience segmentation and align tracking and attribution models.

- Manage internal and agency resources to execute paid social media campaigns.

- Deliver measurable lift in KPIs (CPL, CPA, MQLs, ROAS) through iterative optimization and data-driven experimentation.

- Work in tandem with marketing campaigns lead and product marketing managers to connect user journeys from top-of-funnel through to bottom-of-funnel.

- Communicate strategies and plans to internal and external stakeholders.

### About you:



- Extensive experience in leading data-driven digital campaigns.

- Developing and delivering against end-to-end media plans that include benchmarks and KPIs.

- Feeding into digital content, social media, and integrated marketing plans.

- Previous experience with responsibility for digital campaigns at a B2B and B2C technology company.

Your experience includes:



- Experience running concurrent paid and organic nurture campaigns while simultaneously planning.

### Location:



United States Remote; #LI-Remote United States; This is a fully remote opportunity.

- Seagate is able to offer virtual employment for this position in the following states: AL, AZ, CA, CO, CT, FL, GA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, MA, MD, MI, MN, MO, NC, NH, NJ, NM, NY, NV, OH, OK, OR, PA, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, WI, WY.
- The estimated base salary range for this position is $129,000 - $190,000. The individual salary is based on work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.
- Seagate offers comprehensive benefits to its eligible employees, including, but not limited to, eligibility to participate in discretionary bonus program, medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, short-and long-term disability, 401(k), employee stock purchase plan, health savings account, dependent care, and healthcare spending accounts. Seagate also offers paid time off, including 12 holidays, flexible time off provided pursuant to Seagate policy, a minimum of 48 hours of paid sick leave, and 16 weeks of paid parental leave. The benefits for this position are based on a full-time schedule for a full calendar year and may differ depending on work location.

### Location: Fremont USA Research Center

Travel: Up to 10%

Apply for This Job

• Real world innovation at Seagate

results.title: Career Opportunities: Director, U.S. Government Affairs (13945) | Jobflarely
highlights:
# Career Opportunities: Director, U.S. Government Affairs (13945)
[...]
A global leader in mass-capacity data storage, Seagate is deeply connected to some of the most consequential technology and policy issues worldwide, with products that form the backbone for the cloud, AI, and other data-intensive applications. It’s a remarkable time to be at Seagate: the business is accelerating, the technology leadership is deepening, and the policy landscape is evolving rapidly.
[...]
As part of Seagate’s Legal and Government Affairs function, the Government Affairs team takes a business-first approach to policy engagement. We partner with senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders to design and execute proactive government engagement strategies aligned to enterprise priorities, spanning trade and tariff policy, AI and emerging technology, industrial policy and R&D investment, and supply chain security. We also leverage public affairs strategies to shape the company’s reputation and voice through targeted engagement, thought leadership, and strategic communications.
[...]
- Lead cross-functional integration. Build and sustain an operating rhythm across Legal, Finance, Trade, Supply Chain, Operations, R&D, and Communications to embed government engagement in enterprise planning and to drive internal alignment and policy positions to ensure Seagate acts with speed internally and speaks with one voice externally.

- Strategic operator who connects policy to business outcomes. You don’t just track what’s happening in Washington — you see how political and regulatory developments create risk, opportunity, and competitive advantage for a global technology company. You think in terms of enterprise impact.

- You’re energized by working at the intersection of technology, geopolitics, and corporate strategy — and you’re looking for a role where that work is directly tied to enterprise outcomes.
[...]
- Your experience includes:

Experience in industry, government, trade associations, or advisory environments all bring value — what matters most is demonstrated fluency in how policy engagement connects to corporate priorities and business outcomes.

[...]
- Deep fluency in U.S. federal policy processes, particularly across trade and tariff policy, emerging technology issues, industrial and R&D incentives, and supply chain–related policy.
[...]
- You might also have:
[...]
- Experience in technology hardware, semiconductor, advanced manufacturing, or other trade-exposed industries.
[...]
- Experience engaging directly with Executive Branch stakeholders — including Commerce, USTR, Treasury, the White House, OSTP, etc.
[...]
- Experience navigating incentive programs, public-private partnerships, or government funding mechanisms.

- Familiarity with export control regimes, tariff frameworks, or other trade processes.

- Familiarity with how U.S. policy intersects with international trade dynamics, allied technology frameworks, or geopolitical competition in markets relevant to data storage supply chains.

Experience with public affairs strategies in a Washington
[...]
context — including media engagement, thought
[...]
, or strategic communications.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
in machine learning
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

results.title: Staff Engineer - AI Platform | Hirequorum
highlights:
# Staff Engineer - AI Platform
[...]
At Teradata, we believe that people thrive when empowered with better information. That’s why we built the most complete cloud analytics and data platform for AI. By delivering harmonized data, trusted AI, and faster innovation, we uplift and empower our customers—and our customers’ customers—to make better, more confident decisions. The world’s top companies across every major industry trust Teradata to improve business performance, enrich customer experiences, and fully integrate data across the enterprise.
[...]
At Teradata, we're not just managing data; we're unleashing its full potential. Our ClearScape Analytics™ platform and pioneering Enterprise Vector Store are empowering the world's largest enterprises to derive unprecedented value from their most complex data. We're rapidly pushing the boundaries of what's possible with Artificial Intelligence, especially in the exciting realm of autonomous and agentic systems.
[...]
We’re building intelligent systems that go far beyond automation — they observe, reason, adapt, and drive complex decision-making across large-scale enterprise environments. As a member of our AI engineering team, you’ll play a critical role in designing and deploying advanced AI agents that integrate deeply with business operations, turning data into insight, action, and measurable outcomes.
[...]
In this role, you’ll architect foundational components for production-grade AI systems — from agent frameworks and LLM pipelines to observability and evaluation layers that ensure reliability, accountability, and performance. You’ll be responsible not just for building models, but for making them measurable, debuggable, and trustworthy in real-world, high-stakes deployments.
[...]
You’ll work alongside a high-caliber team of AI researchers, engineers, and data scientists tackling some of the hardest problems in AI and enterprise software — from scalable multi-agent coordination and fine-tuned LLM applications, to real-time monitoring, drift detection, and closed-loop retraining systems.
[...]
Imagine collaborating daily with some of the brightest minds in the company – individuals who champion diversity, equity, and inclusion as fundamental to our success. You'll be part of a cohesive force, laser-focused on delivering high-quality, critical, and highly visible AI/ML functionality within the Teradata Vantage platform. Your insights will directly shape the future of our intelligent data solutions.

- We’re seeking a hands-on, deeply technical Principal Engineer to lead innovation in the Agentic AI space.

- You bring proven experience in the design, development, and deployment of intelligent agents that can reason, plan, and act autonomously—and critically, you’ve done so securely and responsibly at scale.

results.title: Seagate Technology Stock Surges on AI Demand, Analysts Maintain Strong Buy Ratings for 2026
highlights:
# Seagate Technology Stock Surges on AI Demand, Analysts Maintain Strong Buy Ratings for 2026
[...]
Seagate Technology Stock Surges on AI Demand, Analysts Maintain Strong Buy Ratings for 2026
[...]
Technology Stock Surges on AI Demand, Analysts Maintain Strong
[...]
for 2026
[...]
Seagate Technology Holdings plc shares have delivered exceptional returns in 2026, fueled by robust demand for high-capacity data storage solutions driven by artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion, prompting analysts to maintain overwhelmingly positive recommendations and raise price targets amid record margins and revenue growth.
[...]
The storage solutions leader, known for its hard disk drives essential to cloud computing and hyperscale data centers, reported strong fiscal third-quarter 2026 results in late April, with revenue reaching $3.11 billion, up 44% year-over-year, and non-GAAP diluted earnings per share of $4.10, exceeding expectations. Gross margins hit record levels around 47%, reflecting operational efficiency and favorable product mix shifts toward higher-value AI-related storage.
[...]
Seagate's performance benefits from the explosive growth in AI workloads, which require massive amounts of storage for training and inference. The company's heat-assisted magnetic recording (HAMR) technology positions it as a leader in next-generation high-density drives, offering advantages in capacity and cost per terabyte that competitors struggle to match at scale.
[...]
Recent analyst actions underscore confidence. Mizuho raised its price target to $1,090 from $875, while BofA increased to $1,000 from $900 and Citi to $1,150 from $740, citing strengthening storage demand and margin outlook. Barclays and Wells Fargo also lifted targets, reflecting sector-wide optimism around AI tailwinds.
[...]
"Seagate is poised for continued growth with strong demand in both Data Center and Edge/IoT revenue, as well as the implementation of HAMR technology," analysts noted in consensus commentary.
[...]
For investors weighing buy or sell decisions in the second half of 2026, the bull case centers on sustained AI investment by major cloud providers. Hyperscalers like Amazon, Microsoft and Google are expected to ramp data center builds, driving multi-year demand for Seagate's enterprise drives. Analysts project further revenue growth and margin stability as HAMR volumes increase.
[...]
Longer-term forecasts for 2026 and beyond remain constructive. Some models project prices exceeding $1,000, contingent on execution and market conditions. The next earnings report, expected in late July, will provide further clarity on guidance and AI momentum.
[...]
Seagate's strategic focus on mass-capacity storage aligns with secular trends in cloud, big data and machine learning. The company's Singapore headquarters and global operations support efficient manufacturing, while investments in research and development bolster its technological edge.
[...]
Seagate Technology's transformation from a traditional hard drive manufacturer to a key enabler of the AI revolution has resonated with investors and analysts alike. With strong fundamentals, technological leadership and favorable secular trends, the consensus leans toward buying or holding shares for 2026, though careful monitoring of execution and market dynamics is advised.
[...]
As the company prepares for its fiscal fourth quarter and full-year results, attention will focus on whether AI momentum can offset any cyclical pressures. For now, the trajectory points to continued strength in a data-hungry world.

generation (RAG), vector stores, and developer facing AI systems.   You will operate with a high degree of autonomy, partner closely with product and architecture leadership, and raise the technical observability, evaluation, and production readiness of AI systems.   Identify systemic performance bottlenecks and lead efforts to optimize distributed systems and AI pipelines.   Establish engineering standards that improve development velocity, quality, and operational resilience.   Mentorship & Org Level Impact   Mentor Senior and Staff level engineers, providing guidance on architecture, design reviews, and technical decision making.   Raise the overall engineering bar through design forums, technical reviews, and knowledge sharing.   Lead by example through hands on contributions to the most complex and business critical problems.   Required Qualifications (Non negotiable)   Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.   12+ years of experience building backend services, distributed systems, or data/AI platforms.   Strong proficiency in Java, Go, or Python, with experience building largescale services.   Deep understanding of distributed system design, scalability, fault tolerance, and cloud native architectures.   Proven experience designing and operating production systems with SQL and NoSQL data stores.   Preferred / Differentiating Skills   Experience with LLMs, embeddings, vector databases, and AI orchestration frameworks.   Exposure to agentic AI patterns such as tool calling, planning, memory, and multi-step reasoning.   Experience building or operating AI systems in cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP).   Familiarity with Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and production-grade observability.   Why We Think You'll Love Teradata  We prioritize a people-first culture because we know our people are at the very heart of our success. We embrace a flexible work model because we trust our people to make decisions about how, when, and where they work. We focus on well-being because we care

highlights:
Overview: Principal Data Scientist - FSP – Remote (UK/EU) As a Principal Data Scientist, you will hold a combined project leadership and hands-on technical role. Working to develop LLM-powered automation tools for clinical reporting and document generation workflows.
[...]
## Responsibilities: Develop LLM-powered automation tools for clinical reporting and document generation workflows Build AI-driven code generation pipelines and quality assessment frameworks Design and implement human-in-the-loop review workflows with feedback loops to continuously improve output quality Design and build multi-agent systems for data workflows — agents that retrieve, generate, validate, and iterate autonomously Implement agent orchestration using frameworks such as Google ADK, LangGraph, or LangChain Deploy and manage agents on Google Vertex AI Use AI coding tools (e.g. Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot) as a core part of your development workflow Critically review and validate AI-generated code — understanding what it produces, why, and when it’s wrong Write effective prompts to direct AI tools toward correct, secure, well-structured output Know when to use AI and when to write code manually
[...]
## Qualifications: Strong Python skills Hands-on experience building RAG systems or LLM-powered applications (using LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar frameworks) Experience integrating LLM APIs (Google Gemini, OpenAI, or similar) — we work primarily through Google Vertex AI Working knowledge of vector databases (ChromaDB, Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, or similar) Docker and containerized deployments Comfortable using AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot) — and critically evaluating what they produce About Phastar Phastar is an award-winning biometrics and data science CRO, trusted by pharma, biotech, and medical device companies worldwide. With a global team of data specialists, we bring expertise, precision, and pace to every trial, because behind every data point is a patient waiting for treatment. We transform complex data into clear, actionable intelligence, helping accelerate drug development, and bring life-changing therapies to patients faster. Awards & Recognition SCRIP – Best Contract Research Organization, Specialist Provider Citeline – 2025 Award Winner Fierce CRO Awards – Recognized Industry Leader Apply Now Be part of a team known for technical expertise, quality, and impact. We value Excellence, Collaboration, Integrity, Innovation, and Passion—and we seek team members who embody these qualities. Apply today! Phastar is committed to the principles and practices of equal opportunities and to encourage the establishment of a diverse workforce. It is our policy to employ individuals on the basis of their suitability for the work to be performed and their potential for development, regardless of age, sex, race, color, nationality, ethnic or national origin, disability, marital status, pregnancy or maternity, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion, or belief. This includes creating a culture that fully reflects our commitment to equal opportunities for all. Important notice to Employment businesses/Agencies Phastar does not accept referrals from employment businesses and/or employment agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. All employment businesses/agencies are required to contact Phastar's Head of Talent Acquisition to obtain prior written authorization before referring any candidates to Phastar. results.title: Seagate CEO drops eye-opening AI data demand message | STX - TheStreet
highlights:
Seagate CEO drops eye-opening AI data demand message | STX - TheStreet
[...]
# Seagate CEO drops eye-opening message on exploding AI data demand
[...]
Most people think of Artificial Intelligence(AI) as a computing story. Chips, GPUs, data centers. The hardware that processes the intelligence.
[...]
Seagate CEO Dave Mosley wants investors to think about what comes after the processing. All that AI activity — every model trained, every inference run, every video analyzed, every agent deployed — generates data. Enormous, persistent, compounding quantities of data that need to live somewhere.
[...]
That somewhere is Seagate’s business. And Mosley just told Bank of America’s clients something worth paying attention to.
[...]
The central argument Mosley made in the BofA client call, as reported by Investing.com, is straightforward but easy to underappreciate in a market fixated on semiconductor stocks.
[...]
Data is an increasingly valuable asset. And the sources generating it are multiplying simultaneously. Video. Unstructured data. Robotics. Physical AI systems that need to record and process real-world environments.
[...]
Agentic AI architectures that create data snapshots, checkpoints, and redundancy requirements at scale. Each of these represents a structural, not cyclical, driver of storage demand.
[...]
Mosley described the monetization opportunities associated with incremental storage as far outweighing the costs. That is a margin argument that is showing up in Seagate’s financial results.
[...]
The CEO also flagged that strong demand is not just coming from hyperscalers. Traditional enterprise data centers and edge applications are contributing meaningfully, broadening the demand base beyond a handful of cloud giants.
[...]
“Seagate delivered outstanding March quarter results, exceeding the high end of our revenue and EPS guidance, achieving record margin performance, and generating close to $1 billion in free cash flow,” Mosley said in the release.
[...]
## The Mozaic platform is the technical engine behind
[...]
’s margin and capacity story
[...]
The product story underlying Seagate’s financial momentum centers on the Mozaic platform — the company’s Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording technology that enables dramatically higher storage density per drive.
[...]
Seagate has now qualified Mozaic 3 at all planned cloud service providers, and two customers are already qualified on Mozaic 4, according to a Seagate statement. Full qualification across hyperscalers removes the sales friction that characterized the early ramp and allows Seagate to deliver at volume.
[...]
customers in a specific
[...]
, and capital
[...]
BofA reiterated its Buy rating on secular demand from cloud, revenue and margin improvement, and the path to even higher capacity HAMR drives in its note. Mozaic 4 and beyond represent the roadmap that extends Seagate‘s technology advantage.
[...]
## What the AI storage supercycle means for investors
[...]
If Mosley’s characterization of AI as an amplifier of data creation is correct and the quarterly results support it, then Seagate is no longer a commodity storage business.
[...]
It is essential infrastructure for the AI era, with improving margins, a differentiated technology roadmap, and a demand tailwind, with CEO, analyst, and hyperscaler procurement decisions all pointing in the same direction.
[...]
The $900 BofA target implies modest upside from the current level of $845. The more important question is whether the structural demand thesis Mosley described holds through 2027 and beyond. And whether Seagate’s Mozaic platform maintains its density and pricing advantages as competitors respond. So far, the evidence is running in Seagate’s favor.
